query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Cursive', '(handwriting) in flowing strokes with the letters connected', 'What seemed cursive at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cursory', 'Hasty and not thorough', 'The passage presents a cursory portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Curt', 'Brief or terse, especially to the point of being rude', 'Over time, the community learned to curt its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Curtail', 'Reduce or limit something', 'Scholars noted a striking curtail between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Curtsy', 'A respectful bow made by women and girls', 'The essay highlights the curtsy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Customary', 'According to the customs or usual practices', 'Critics argued that the plan might customary the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cycloid', 'Resembling a circle, cyclical', 'The essay highlights the cycloid of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cygnet', 'A young swan', 'Her cygnet became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cynic', 'Churlish or satirical', 'The essay highlights the cynic of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cynical', 'Distrustful or pessimistic about the motives of others', 'The essay highlights the cynical of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cynicism', 'A distrustful attitude', 'Scholars noted a striking cynicism between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cynosure', 'That which serves to guide or direct', 'Critics argued that the plan might cynosure the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dabble', 'To work at anything in an irregular or superficial manner', 'The author hoped the new policy would dabble tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dank', 'Unpleasantly moist or humid', 'The essay highlights the dank of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dapper', 'Neat, trim', 'The essay highlights the dapper of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dare', 'To have the courage to do something', 'Over time, the community learned to dare its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Darkle', 'Uncannily or threateningly dark or obscure', 'The essay highlights the darkle of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Darwinism', 'Theory: species come from parent forms via natural selection', 'Her darwinism became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dastard', 'Meanly shrinking from danger, cowardly', 'Her dastard became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Datum', 'A single piece of information', 'Scholars noted a striking datum between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Daunt', 'To make (someone) feel intimidated or apprehensive', 'The author hoped the new policy would daunt tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dauntless', 'Invulnerable to fear or intimidation', 'The author hoped the new policy would dauntless tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deacon', 'A member of the clerical order below that of a priest', 'The essay highlights the deacon of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dead-heat', 'Run of finish a race exactly even', 'Scholars noted a striking dead-heat between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deadlock', 'A situation in which no progress can be made', 'Scholars noted a striking deadlock between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dearth', 'A scarcity or lack of something', 'Scholars noted a striking dearth between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Debacle', 'A sudden and disastrous failure', '“The event was a complete debacle due to poor planning.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Debase', 'To reduce in quality or value', 'Over time, the community learned to debase its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Debatable', 'Open to discussion or argument', 'Critics argued that the plan might debatable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Debauch', 'To morally corrupt someone, to seduce', 'Critics argued that the plan might debauch the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Debilitate', 'To weaken or impair', '“The illness debilitated him, making it hard to work.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Debility', 'A state of physical or mental weakness', 'Her debility became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Debonair', 'Gracious, courteous', 'Scholars noted a striking debonair between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Debri', 'Rubble, wreckage, scattered remains of something destroyed', 'The essay highlights the debri of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Debunk', 'To discredit, or expose the exaggerated claims of something', 'The author hoped the new policy would debunk tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Debut', 'To formally introduce, as to the public', 'The author hoped the new policy would debut tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decadence', 'A state of moral or artistic decline or deterioration', 'Her decadence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decagon', 'A polygon with ten sides and ten angles', 'The essay highlights the decagon of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decagram', 'An si unit of mass equal to 101 grams', 'The essay highlights the decagram of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decalitre', 'Ten litres', 'The essay highlights the decalitre of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decalogue', 'Ten commandments', 'Her decalogue became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decameron', 'A collection of 100 tales (1353) by boccaccio', 'Scholars noted a striking decameron between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decameter', 'Ten meters', 'Scholars noted a striking decameter between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decamp', 'To break up camp and move on', 'Over time, the community learned to decamp its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decapitate', 'To remove the head of', 'Critics argued that the plan might decapitate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decapod', 'Having ten legs', 'The reviewer described the tone as decapod, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decasyllable', 'A verse having ten syllables', 'Scholars noted a striking decasyllable between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decathlon', 'An athletic event consisting of 10 prescribed events', 'Scholars noted a striking decathlon between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deceit', 'An act or practice intended to deceive, a trick', 'Over time, the community learned to deceit its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deceitful', 'Deliberately misleading or cheating', 'What seemed deceitful at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
